taxonomy: Blakwellia foetida is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae. It belongs to the genus Blakwellia, which is a monotypic genus. The family Boraginaceae is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as borage or forget-me-not family. This family includes around 2,000 species spread across 200 genera. These plants are primarily found in temperate and subtropical regions around the world.
uses: Blakwellia foetida has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, including the treatment of skin conditions and wounds. Its antibacterial and antifungal properties make it effective in combating infections. The plant is also used to treat fever and respiratory issues. In some regions, Blakwellia foetida is used in traditional medicine to treat snake bites and other poisonous stings.
